articles.title: Indonesia Posts 0.93% Budget Deficit in Q1
articles.expand: Indonesia recorded a budget deficit of IDR 240.1 trillion, equivalent to 0.93% of GDP, in the first quarter of 2026, Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa announced on Monday. Government expenditure surged 31.3% year-on-year to IDR 815 trillion, while state revenue increased 10.5% to IDR 574.9 trillion. Revenue performance was underpinned by tax re...

articles.expand: Philip Wee at DBS Group Research argues that USD/JPY looks overextended as it tests Japan’s 160 pain threshold. The pair is supported by the US-Japan rate differential, but markets now price a roughly two-thirds chance of a Bank of Japan (BoJ) rate hike on April 28.
